entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} are practically inseparable these days.
details: You see one, the other's not far behind. They've got their own shorthand and inside jokes already.
I don't know when it started, but they've become a unit.
reactions: Good for them. Everyone needs someone they can count on.

entries.intro: {npc_name} complains about everything, and I mean everything.
details: Too hot, too cold, too early, too late. The bread's too dry, the water's too wet.
I timed it once: four complaints before finishing a cup of tea.
reactions: I've started treating it like weather. It's just background noise at this point.

statCheck.pass: I think complaining is how they process the world. It's not anger: it's narration. Everything gets a critique.
statCheck.fail: I have no idea why they do it. Some people just run on grievance.

entries.intro: {npc_name} remembered my birthday when I'd forgotten it myself.
details: Left a little bundle of dried herbs on my doorstep with a note. Nothing big, but thoughtful.
I didn't even know they knew when it was.
reactions: It's the noticing that matters more than the gift.

entries.intro: I don't think {npc_name} likes me very much, and honestly, the feeling is mutual.
details: We're civil. We nod. We keep it short. That's about the best either of us can manage.
It's been that way since we disagreed about the well placement. Some grudges just settle in.
reactions: We don't have to be friends. We just have to coexist.

entries.intro: {npc_name} was singing while washing clothes this morning. Caught me off guard.
details: I don't think they knew anyone was listening. I stood there a bit longer than I needed to.
Old song, something about rivers. The kind your grandmother would know.
reactions: I couldn't tell you if it was good or not. But it stuck with me.

entries.intro: I asked {npc_name} a simple yes-or-no question and got a twenty-minute answer.
details: By the end I'd forgotten what I asked. I think they had too.
It's not that they're boring: they just can't find the off switch once they get going.
reactions: Next time I'll ask when I'm already walking somewhere.

entries.intro: {npc_name} is the first person I'd go to in an emergency. No question about it.
details: Calm head, steady hands, doesn't waste time panicking. They just start solving the problem.
I've seen it twice now and both times they were the reason things didn't get worse.
reactions: You don't know who the reliable ones are until something goes wrong.

entries.intro: {npc_name} has a way of making everything sound like a competition.
details: I mentioned I gathered a full basket of mushrooms and suddenly they'd gathered two last week.
It's exhausting. You can't share good news without it turning into a contest.
reactions: I've started keeping things to myself. It's just easier.

statCheck.pass: I think they're insecure and overcompensate. It's not malice: it's a need to feel capable.
statCheck.fail: I don't know why they do it. Maybe they just can't help themselves.

entries.intro: I found out {npc_name} has been watering my garden plot while I've been laid up. Never mentioned it.
details: I only know because {npc_name_2} saw them out there at dawn and told me.
The plants look better than when I tend them myself, honestly.
reactions: Some kindness is best when it's quiet.

entries.intro: {npc_name} never looks you in the eye when they're talking. Makes me uneasy.
details: Always looking past you, or down, or at something over your shoulder.
I've tried shifting position to catch their gaze. They just shift too.
reactions: Probably just how they are. Not everyone's comfortable with eye contact.

entries.intro: {npc_name} volunteered to take my night watch shift so I could rest. Didn't want anything for it.
details: I'd been up two nights running and it must have shown. They just said 'go sleep, I've got it.'
Came back in the morning and everything was in order. No fuss.
reactions: I won't forget that.

entries.intro: I caught {npc_name} going through the supply shed at a strange hour. I didn't say anything, but I noticed.
details: Could have been perfectly innocent. People need things at odd times.
But they were jumpy when they heard me coming. That part felt off.
reactions: I'm not making accusations. I'm just paying more attention now.

statCheck.pass: I've seen them do it twice now, both times late evening. Whatever they're after, they'd rather no one see.
statCheck.fail: It was dark and I was tired. Might have read the whole thing wrong.

entries.intro: {npc_name} is the kind of person who listens when you talk. Actually listens.
details: They don't interrupt. They don't start thinking about what they're going to say next.
I told them about a problem I was having and a week later they checked in on it. Remembered every detail.
reactions: Most people hear you. {npc_name} pays attention.

entries.intro: {npc_name} borrowed my good knife and returned it duller than a river stone.
details: I spent an hour at the whetstone getting the edge back. An hour I'll never get back.
They handed it over like nothing was wrong. I don't think they even noticed.
reactions: I'm putting my name on my tools from now on. All of them.

statCheck.pass: They were using it to pry something: I can tell from the nick pattern on the edge. That's not what a knife is for.
statCheck.fail: I don't know what they did to it. Doesn't matter: the damage is done.

entries.intro: I've noticed {npc_name} always makes sure the elderly folk have what they need. Quietly, without anyone asking.
details: Carrying water, stacking wood by their doors, checking in after storms.
Most people around here barely notice. But I see it.
reactions: That tells you everything about a person's character.

entries.intro: {npc_name} has a habit of standing too close when they talk to you.
details: I take a step back, they take a step forward. We've crossed entire rooms this way.
I don't think they realize they're doing it.
reactions: I've started having conversations near walls. Nowhere left to advance.

entries.intro: {npc_name} told {npc_name_2} something I said in confidence. I found out the hard way.
details: {npc_name_2} brought it up casually like everyone already knew. My stomach dropped.
It wasn't anything terrible, but that's not the point.
reactions: Trust broken is trust broken, no matter how small the secret.

entries.intro: {npc_name} takes tea very seriously. I've never seen someone put that much thought into a cup.
details: There's something about the way they brew it: slow, patient, always the right temperature.
I've tried to copy it at home and it's never the same.
reactions: I can't tell if it's a skill or just a personality trait at this point.

entries.intro: {npc_name} acts differently around {npc_name_2} than everyone else. I've noticed.
details: With the rest of us it's all confidence and big talk. Around {npc_name_2} they go quiet and fidgety.
It's been going on for weeks. I'm not the only one who's picked up on it.
reactions: Not my business. But it's hard not to notice.

statCheck.pass: I think they admire {npc_name_2} and don't quite know how to show it. It comes out as awkwardness.
statCheck.fail: No idea what's behind it. People are strange sometimes.

entries.intro: {npc_name} always volunteers for the worst jobs. The muddy, cold, thankless ones.
details: Ditch clearing, roof patching in the rain, hauling waste. Never complains about any of it.
I asked why once and they just shrugged and said somebody has to.
reactions: I respect that more than all the big talk from people who never lift a finger.

entries.intro: {npc_name} has been avoiding me and I have no idea why.
details: They changed direction twice when they saw me coming. Both times they pretended to be going somewhere else.
I went over what I've said and done recently and nothing stands out.
reactions: If I did something wrong, I'd rather just hear it.

statCheck.pass: I noticed it started right after the gathering last week. Maybe something happened there that I missed.
statCheck.fail: I've got nothing. Completely in the dark.

entries.intro: {npc_name} sat with me during that thunderstorm when the roof was leaking and we couldn't sleep.
details: We just talked about nothing for hours. Childhood, food we missed, places we'd never been.
By morning the storm had passed and I felt like I'd known them my whole life.
reactions: Some friendships start in the strangest moments.

entries.intro: {npc_name} has this way of sighing that makes you feel like you've disappointed them personally.
details: It's a deep one, from the chest. Usually aimed at whatever you've just said.
I don't think they know they do it. Or maybe they do and that's worse.
reactions: I've started second-guessing myself before I even open my mouth around them.

statCheck.pass: It's a habit from their upbringing, I think. Where they grew up, sighing was just how people expressed thought: not disapproval.
statCheck.fail: No idea if it's intentional. Either way, the effect is the same.

entries.intro: When things go sideways, {npc_name} is the one cracking jokes to keep everyone steady.
details: Not in a dismissive way. More like they know people need something to hold onto besides worry.
Last time the creek flooded, they had us laughing while we sandbagged. Made it bearable.
reactions: Humor like that is a kind of strength. Most people don't see it that way, but it is.

entries.intro: {npc_name} chews with their mouth open and it's all I can hear at mealtimes.
details: I've moved seats. I've tried eating at different times. They always end up nearby.
Nobody else seems bothered. I might be losing my mind.
reactions: It's such a stupid thing to be annoyed about. And yet.

entries.intro: I used to think {npc_name} didn't like me. Now I think they're just shy.
details: Once we actually talked, properly talked, I realized the coldness was probably just nerves.
We get along better now. But it took me longer to figure them out than most.
reactions: First impressions aren't always the right ones. I try to remember that.

entries.intro: {npc_name} cracks their knuckles constantly. I mean constantly.
details: Every finger, one by one, then both hands at once. Multiple times an hour.
I flinch every single time. They find that funny, which doesn't help.
reactions: Small habits shouldn't bother you this much. But some do.

entries.intro: I ran into {npc_name} at the worst moment of my week. They said exactly the right thing.
details: Nothing grand. Just 'it'll keep' and a firm nod. But somehow it was enough.
I don't even think they knew how bad my day was. Some people just sense it.
reactions: The right words at the right time can carry you further than you'd think.

entries.intro: {npc_name} agreed to split the cost of new fencing with me and then backed out.
details: Said the price was too high after we'd already agreed. Left me holding the full bill.
The fence still needs building. I'm doing it alone now.
reactions: An agreement should mean something. Otherwise what are words for?

entries.intro: {npc_name} always waves when they see me. Every single time, without fail.
details: Across the road, through a window, from the other end of the field. Always a wave.
I'm not sure if they do it with everyone or if it's just me.
reactions: I wave back. What else are you going to do?

entries.intro: I heard {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} had a shouting match behind the storehouse.
details: Didn't catch what it was about, but you could hear them from the road.
By evening they were sitting together like nothing happened. I'll never understand those two.
reactions: Some people fight loud and forgive fast. Better than holding grudges, I suppose.

entries.intro: {npc_name} takes everything personally. You can't give the mildest feedback without it becoming a crisis.
details: I suggested a different way to hang the lanterns and you'd have thought I insulted their family.
Spent the rest of the day barely speaking to anyone.
reactions: I've learned to phrase things very, very carefully around them.

statCheck.pass: I think they tie their self-worth to their work. Any criticism of the work feels like criticism of them.
statCheck.fail: I don't know why they're so sensitive. Some people just are.

entries.intro: There's something steady about {npc_name}. Like a tree that's been there longer than the path.
details: They don't get rattled. Good news, bad news: same calm face, same measured response.
When everyone else is running around in circles, {npc_name} is the one standing still and thinking.
reactions: Steadiness like that is undervalued. Until you need it.

entries.intro: {npc_name} only talks to you when they need something. I've been keeping track.
details: Three conversations this month. Every one ended with a favor being asked.
Between those it's like I don't exist. I could be a fence post.
reactions: Next time they come around with that friendly opening, I'll know what's coming.

entries.intro: {npc_name} walked me home in the dark last week when the path was icy. Wouldn't let me go alone.
details: I said I'd be fine and they said 'probably, but I'm going that way anyway.' They weren't.
We walked in comfortable silence most of the way.
reactions: Small kindnesses like that stick with you.

entries.intro: {npc_name} leaves their things everywhere. The common areas look like a storm hit.
details: Boots by the door, tools on the bench, a coat draped over something new every day.
I moved a pair of gloves once and got asked why I was touching their stuff.
reactions: Shared space means shared responsibility. Apparently not everyone got that message.

statCheck.pass: They spread out because they're always mid-task on three things at once. The mess is a map of their day.
statCheck.fail: I can't explain it. Some people just don't see clutter.

entries.intro: I genuinely look forward to seeing {npc_name} each day. They brighten the place up.
details: Always a kind word, always interested in how you're doing. And they mean it: you can tell.
Some mornings just hearing them say 'good morning' properly resets my whole mood.
reactions: People who spread warmth without trying are rare. I don't take it for granted.

entries.intro: {npc_name} exaggerates everything. A light rain becomes a flood, a scratch becomes a wound.
details: Last week a cat knocked over a cup and to hear them tell it, the whole shelf came down.
You learn to divide every story by three and you're closer to the truth.
reactions: They're entertaining, I'll give them that. Just not reliable as a source.

entries.intro: {npc_name} taught me how to tie a proper knot last month. I still use it.
details: Showed me three times. Patient about it, which I didn't expect.
It's one of those things: simple once you know it, but someone has to show you.
reactions: Funny how a small skill from a random afternoon sticks around longer than most conversations.

entries.intro: {npc_name} has been playing favorites and I'm clearly not on the list.
details: Certain people get help, certain people get smiles. The rest of us get leftovers.
I noticed whose roof got patched first after the last storm. Wasn't random.
reactions: Fair treatment shouldn't depend on who you eat lunch with.

statCheck.pass: It's not deliberate cruelty: it's just that some people are in the inner circle and some aren't. Doesn't make it fair.
statCheck.fail: Maybe I'm reading too much into it. Hard to know from the outside.

entries.intro: {npc_name} gave me advice once that completely changed how I approach my work.
details: It was offhand, not even a proper conversation. But it solved a problem I'd been wrestling with for weeks.
I don't think they even remember saying it.
reactions: Funny how the biggest insights come from the smallest moments.

entries.intro: I'm pretty sure {npc_name} ate the last of the preserved fruit and blamed it on animals.
details: The jar was on a high shelf. No animal I know has thumbs and a stool.
I didn't press it. But I'm not making more until I get a straight answer.
reactions: Just own up to it. That's all I ask.

statCheck.pass: There were crumbs on the worktable and {npc_name}'s cup was still wet. The timing fits perfectly.
statCheck.fail: I can't prove it. But I know what I know.

entries.intro: {npc_name} is one of those rare people who apologizes when they're wrong. Actually means it too.
details: No hedging, no 'sorry if you felt that way.' Just a plain 'I was wrong, I'm sorry.'
It happened once over a scheduling mix-up and I've respected them more ever since.
reactions: That kind of honesty takes more courage than most people realize.

entries.intro: {npc_name} has a different excuse every time something doesn't get done.
details: Monday it's the weather. Tuesday it's their back. By the end of the week they've run out of reasons and just shrug.
The work still needs doing. Excuses don't patch roofs.
reactions: At some point you stop asking and start planning around them.

entries.intro: I used to keep my distance from {npc_name}. Now I'd call them a friend.
details: Took a while. We rubbed each other wrong at first: different habits, different rhythms.
Then one day we ended up stuck waiting out rain under the same overhang, and something shifted.
reactions: Some of the best friendships aren't instant. They take seasoning.

entries.intro: {npc_name} watches everyone. Not in a threatening way, just... always observing.
details: You'll look up and find them sitting there, taking it all in. Quiet as a cat on a windowsill.
I asked what they're thinking about once and they said 'nothing in particular.' I doubt that.
reactions: I can't decide if it's comforting or unsettling. Depends on the day.

entries.intro: {npc_name} wakes up before everyone else. I've never once beaten them to the well.
details: Dawn's barely cracking and they've already got water drawn and a fire going.
I tried getting up early to surprise them once. They were already done.
reactions: I don't know when they sleep. Maybe they don't.

statCheck.pass: I've seen a candle in their window at odd hours. I think they sleep in short bursts, not one long stretch.
statCheck.fail: No idea how they manage it. Some people just need less sleep.

entries.intro: {npc_name} sided with {npc_name_2} over me during the path dispute, and I haven't forgotten.
details: I laid out my reasoning clear as day. {npc_name} just nodded along and then voted the other way.
It's not about the path. It's about not being heard.
reactions: We're civil. But there's a distance now that wasn't there before.

entries.intro: {npc_name} shares their harvest without keeping score. I've never seen them ask for anything back.
details: Good season, bad season: there's always something at your door if you need it.
I've tried to repay them and they wave it off like it's nothing.
reactions: Generosity without conditions is the rarest kind.

entries.intro: {npc_name} corrected me in front of everyone yesterday. Could have pulled me aside.
details: I know I was wrong about the measurements. That's not the issue.
The issue is making someone feel small in front of others when a quiet word would do.
reactions: I'll get over it. But I'll remember how it felt.

entries.intro: You know what I like about {npc_name}? They admit when they don't know something.
details: No bluffing, no making things up. Just a straight 'I don't know' and then they go find out.
In a world of people pretending to have answers, that's refreshing.
reactions: Honesty about ignorance is its own kind of wisdom.

entries.intro: {npc_name} snores so loud you can hear it through the walls. I'm losing sleep over it.
details: I've tried stuffing cloth in my ears. I've tried sleeping at the other end of the house. Nothing works.
Brought it up once and they said they don't snore. I wanted to laugh.
reactions: If I look tired, now you know why.

statCheck.pass: It gets worse when they sleep on their back. If someone nudged them to roll over it would stop. But nobody wants that job.
statCheck.fail: I have no remedy. I've tried everything short of moving.

entries.intro: {npc_name} defended me when people were talking behind my back. I only heard about it later.
details: Someone was saying unfair things and {npc_name} shut it down. Didn't even tell me they'd done it.
I found out through someone else who was there.
reactions: That's loyalty. The real kind, not the kind that needs an audience.

entries.intro: I'm fairly sure {npc_name} is the reason my fence keeps getting knocked loose.
details: It happens on the side closest to their property. Same boards, every time.
I've repaired it three times this month. At some point that stops being coincidence.
reactions: I'm not starting a fight over fence boards. But I'm watching.

statCheck.pass: Their goat gets out at night: I've seen the tracks. It's probably the goat, not {npc_name} themselves.
statCheck.fail: Could be weather, could be animals. Could be carelessness. I just don't know.

entries.intro: {npc_name} can tell what's wrong with you before you've said a word. It's uncanny.
details: I walked in feeling off and before I sat down they said 'rough night?' Just from looking at me.
They do it with everyone. It's like they read faces the way some people read tracks.
reactions: Part of me finds it comforting. Part of me finds it alarming.

statCheck.pass: They watch body language closely: the way you walk, how you hold your shoulders. That's how they do it.
statCheck.fail: I have no idea how they know. Some people are just perceptive.

entries.intro: I think {npc_name} is lonely but too proud to say so.
details: They linger at the end of conversations like they don't want them to stop.
I've noticed them sitting alone more often than not, always looking like they're about to get up and join someone but never quite doing it.
reactions: Maybe I should make more of an effort. It costs nothing to sit down next to someone.

statCheck.pass: Pride and loneliness feed each other. The more isolated you get, the harder it is to reach out.
statCheck.fail: Could be they just like their own company. Not everyone who sits alone is lonely.

entries.intro: {npc_name} left a nasty comment about my cooking and pretended it was a joke.
details: Said the stew tasted like pond water and laughed like that made it fine.
Everyone else went quiet. That tells you it wasn't funny.
reactions: Call it what it is. A joke is something both people laugh at.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and I don't agree on much, but I respect them all the same.
details: We see things differently on almost everything: how to run things, what matters, what doesn't.
But they're honest about where they stand and they don't play games. That counts for a lot.
reactions: Disagreement doesn't have to mean disrespect.

entries.intro: {npc_name} whistles the same three notes over and over while they work. It's burned into my brain.
details: Three notes up, pause, three notes up again. All day long.
I caught myself doing it in the bath yesterday. I'm infected.
reactions: If I start whistling near you, I apologize in advance.

entries.intro: I misjudged {npc_name} when they first arrived. I'm still not sure I've got them figured out.
details: They seemed standoffish at first. Turns out they were just overwhelmed and didn't know anyone.
We get along better now, but I still can't tell what they're thinking half the time.
reactions: First impressions lie more often than they tell the truth. I try to keep that in mind.

entries.intro: {npc_name} talks to everyone like they're slightly stupid. I don't think they realize it.
details: Slow words, small ideas, always explaining things you already understand.
It's not just me. I've watched them do it to people twice as experienced.
reactions: One day someone less patient than me is going to call them on it.

statCheck.pass: I think they used to teach younger folk wherever they came from. The habit stuck: they talk to everyone like students.
statCheck.fail: No idea where it comes from. Maybe they just think they're the smartest person in the room.

entries.intro: I asked {npc_name} to teach me how they do their leatherwork. They spent the whole afternoon showing me.
details: Didn't rush, didn't hold anything back. Let me make mistakes and then showed me why they happened.
I'm nowhere near their level, but I can do the basics now. That's more than I had yesterday.
reactions: People who teach willingly leave something behind that lasts.

entries.intro: {npc_name} never owns up to mistakes. It's always somebody else's fault.
details: The gate was left open and we lost a day's work rounding up animals. Somehow that was my problem.
I was there. I saw who left it open. It wasn't me.
reactions: Accountability costs nothing. But apparently it's too expensive for some.

entries.intro: {npc_name} always seems to know who needs a break. They just appear with two cups and a place to sit.
details: No big announcement. They just show up and say 'take a minute' like it's the most natural thing.
I've seen them do it for people they barely know.
reactions: Noticing when someone's running on empty is a skill. {npc_name} has it in spades.

entries.intro: I'm starting to think {npc_name} enjoys arguing more than being right.
details: They'll take the opposite side of anything you say, just to see where it goes.
I tested it once: changed my position halfway through. They changed theirs too.
reactions: It's sport to them. I find it tiring.

entries.intro: {npc_name} left wildflowers on the communal table this morning. No note, no claim.
details: Just a rough little bundle in a clay cup, sitting there for no apparent reason.
I don't know for certain it was them, but I saw dirt on their hands at breakfast.
reactions: I noticed it. Wasn't sure what to make of it.

entries.intro: {npc_name} makes promises like they're free. I've learned to wait and see.
details: 'I'll be there.' 'I'll take care of it.' 'You can count on me.' All said with total confidence.
Maybe half of them actually happen.
reactions: I judge people by what they do, not what they say they'll do.

entries.intro: When my tools were stolen, {npc_name} lent me theirs without a moment's hesitation.
details: Said 'use them as long as you need' and meant it. Wouldn't take payment.
I had them for three weeks and not once did they ask for them back or make me feel bad about it.
reactions: There's generous, and then there's whatever {npc_name} is. A step beyond.

entries.intro: {npc_name} interrupts every conversation. You can't finish a thought around them.
details: You'll be mid-sentence and they jump in like they've been waiting for a gap that wasn't there.
I've tried pausing to let them go first. They take the pause and then interrupt again later anyway.
reactions: I've resorted to talking faster. It doesn't help.

statCheck.pass: It's excitement, not disrespect. Their brain moves faster than the conversation and they can't hold it back.
statCheck.fail: I can't tell if they don't notice or don't care. The result is the same either way.

entries.intro: {npc_name} has this calm about them that puts everyone at ease. Like nothing's ever urgent.
details: Even when something IS urgent, they handle it at the same steady pace. And somehow it works.
I've tried to copy that energy. Lasted about half a morning.
reactions: I envy that composure. Genuinely.

entries.intro: {npc_name} somehow always gets the better end of any deal. Every single time.
details: You walk away thinking it was fair, and then two days later you realize you got the short end.
I don't think they're cheating exactly. They're just better at this than the rest of us.
reactions: I've started bringing someone with me when I trade with them. An extra pair of eyes helps.

statCheck.pass: They anchor the conversation with an extreme starting point, then make the compromise feel generous. Classic technique.
statCheck.fail: I don't know how they do it. They just have a knack.

entries.intro: {npc_name} checks on everyone after a storm. Every door, every household.
details: Doesn't matter how tired they are. They make the rounds before they rest.
Last time they found a blocked chimney nobody else had noticed. Could have gone badly.
reactions: That's not a job anyone gave them. They gave it to themselves.

entries.intro: {npc_name} managed to offend {npc_name_2} and me in the same conversation. That takes talent.
details: Started with an innocent comment about dinner, veered into criticism of our work, and ended with opinions about our housekeeping.
I looked at {npc_name_2} and we both had the same expression.
reactions: At least it brought {npc_name_2} and me closer. Nothing bonds like shared annoyance.

entries.intro: {npc_name} can make anyone laugh. Even people having the worst day of their lives.
details: It's not rehearsed or clever. They just see the absurd side of things and point at it.
I've seen them cheer up people I thought were beyond reaching.
reactions: That kind of gift is worth more than most people realize.

entries.intro: {npc_name} takes credit for things other people did. It's happened more than once.
details: The drainage ditch was {npc_name_2}'s idea from start to finish. But ask {npc_name} and you'd think they planned the whole thing.
I watched it happen in real time and said nothing. I should have.
reactions: Credit matters. People need to know their work is seen.

entries.intro: {npc_name} always saves me a seat when we gather for meals. Small thing, but I notice it.
details: Even when they arrive first and the table fills up, there's a spot with my cup already there.
It's the kind of thoughtfulness that doesn't announce itself.
reactions: Being thought of is a good feeling. I try to return it.

entries.intro: {npc_name} turns every communal decision into a debate that lasts twice as long as it should.
details: We were deciding where to stack the firewood. Should have taken five minutes. Took an hour.
Every option gets a twenty-point analysis. Some decisions just don't need that.
reactions: Thoroughness is a virtue. But so is knowing when good enough is good enough.

entries.intro: {npc_name} noticed I was struggling before I even knew I was. Sat down and asked the right questions.
details: Didn't push. Didn't pry. Just opened a door and waited to see if I'd walk through it.
I ended up talking for an hour. I didn't mean to, but they made it easy.
reactions: Sometimes you don't know you need to talk until someone gives you the space.

entries.intro: {npc_name} is always the last to pitch in and the first to leave when work is done.
details: They drift in after the hard part's over, do the easy bits, and somehow look busy the whole time.
I've mentioned it to others and everyone's noticed. Nobody wants to be the one to say something.
reactions: In a small settlement, everyone sees who's pulling their weight. Everyone.

statCheck.pass: They position themselves where the visible work is. Not the important work: the visible work. It's calculated.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they just don't see the problem. Some people genuinely don't.

entries.intro: {npc_name} carved a little figure for a child here who'd been having a hard time. Never said a word about it.
details: Just left it where the kid would find it. Rough little thing, shaped like a bird.
The kid carries it everywhere now. {npc_name} pretends not to notice.
reactions: Kindness aimed at children tells you everything about a person.

entries.intro: {npc_name} told me one thing and told {npc_name_2} something completely different.
details: We compared stories by accident and neither version matched.
Maybe there's a good explanation. But I can't think of one.
reactions: It's hard to trust someone who gives different answers to different people.

statCheck.pass: They tailor their version to what each person wants to hear. It's people-pleasing, not dishonesty: but the effect is the same.
statCheck.fail: I don't understand why they'd do it. Seems like more trouble than being straight.

entries.intro: {npc_name} always makes sure there's enough for everyone before they take their share.
details: Food, supplies, seats by the fire: they always serve themselves last.
I've watched people not even notice because it's so quiet and consistent.
reactions: The ones who put others first are usually the last to be thanked. That's backwards.

entries.intro: {npc_name} has a cold stare that could freeze a pond. I don't know if they mean to do it.
details: Mid-conversation they'll go blank and just look at you. No expression, no warmth.
Lasts a second or two and then they're back to normal. But that second or two sticks.
reactions: Probably just their thinking face. Still throws me every time.

entries.intro: I think {npc_name} is smarter than they let on. They play it down on purpose.
details: They'll say something offhand that cuts right to the heart of a problem nobody else has solved.
Then they shrug it off like anyone could have seen it.
reactions: I don't know why they hide it. Maybe it's easier than dealing with expectations.

statCheck.pass: People who've been burned for being clever learn to keep it quiet. I think that's what happened.
statCheck.fail: Hard to say whether it's humility or strategy.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and I used to be close. I'm not sure what changed.
details: No big fight, no dramatic falling out. We just drifted. The conversations got shorter, the visits less frequent.
I still wave, they still wave back. But there's a gap there now.
reactions: Not all losses are sudden. Some just slip away while you're not watching.

entries.intro: {npc_name} tells you the truth even when you don't want to hear it. I value that.
details: I showed them a project I was proud of and they pointed out two flaws I'd missed. They were right.
It stung in the moment, but the end result was better because of it.
reactions: People who make you better aren't always the ones who make you feel good.

entries.intro: {npc_name} agreed to trade shifts with me and then claimed they never said that.
details: I had plans. I organized my whole week around it. Then they looked at me like I was making things up.
No witnesses. My word against theirs.
reactions: Next time I'll get it in writing. Or just not bother asking.

entries.intro: {npc_name} has been teaching the younger folk how to mend nets. Patient as stone about it too.
details: I watched for a bit. Same explanation, fourth time, same calm voice. Not a flicker of frustration.
The kids actually listen to them, which is more than most of us can say.
reactions: Patience like that is a gift. Especially with young hands that haven't learned yet.

entries.intro: {npc_name} never asks how I'm doing. Not once in all the time we've been neighbors.
details: They'll talk about the weather, the chores, what needs doing. Never about people.
I've tested it. I volunteer something personal and the subject changes within ten seconds.
reactions: Maybe that's just how they are. But it makes me feel like furniture.

statCheck.pass: I think personal questions feel intrusive to them. They show they care through actions, not words. Different language.
statCheck.fail: I don't understand it. Maybe they're just not curious about people.

entries.intro: {npc_name} gets excited about the oddest things. A sunset, a stone, a particular cloud shape.
details: They'll stop what they're doing just to point something out. 'Look at that,' they'll say, like they've never seen rain before.
I can't always tell if it's genuine wonder or if they're just easily distracted.
reactions: It's different. I'll give them that.

entries.intro: {npc_name} keeps rearranging the common workspace without asking anyone. It's maddening.
details: I put a tool down, turn around, and it's been moved to a 'better' spot that makes no sense.
They think they're organizing. The rest of us can't find anything.
reactions: Shared spaces need shared rules. Not one person's vision imposed on everyone.

entries.intro: I admire how {npc_name} handled the disagreement with the trader last week.
details: Stayed firm without raising their voice. Made their point clearly and walked away when it was done.
Most people either fold or blow up. {npc_name} did neither.
reactions: Standing your ground calmly is harder than people think.

entries.intro: {npc_name} lets their animals wander into everyone's garden. We've all said something. Nothing changes.
details: I found tracks through my herb bed again this morning. Third time this season.
The apology is always sincere. The fence is never fixed.
reactions: Sincerity without follow-through is just noise.

statCheck.pass: The fence post on the north side is rotted through. That's where they escape. A single repair would solve it.
statCheck.fail: I don't know why the animals keep getting out. I just know they do.

entries.intro: {npc_name} said something kind about me to {npc_name_2}, and {npc_name_2} passed it along.
details: It wasn't anything big. Just that I'd been doing good work and they'd noticed.
But hearing that someone says nice things about you behind your back: that's different from hearing it to your face.
reactions: I've been smiling about it all week.

entries.intro: {npc_name} always manages to make group decisions about what they want. Subtle, but effective.
details: They phrase things so the choice they prefer sounds like the obvious one. By the time you notice, everyone's already agreed.
I've pointed it out to others and they say 'no, that was everyone's decision.' It wasn't.
reactions: Influence isn't bad on its own. But it should be transparent.

statCheck.pass: They seed the idea early, then let others arrive at it 'independently.' It's masterful, honestly, even if it bothers me.
statCheck.fail: I can feel it happening but I can't quite describe how they do it.

entries.intro: {npc_name} found my lost cat and carried it home. In the rain. Without complaining.
details: I'd been looking for hours and had given up. They showed up at my door soaking wet, cat under one arm.
Just said 'I think this belongs to you' and handed it over.
reactions: I owe them one. A real one.

entries.intro: {npc_name} is impossible to pin down for a straight answer. It's like catching fog.
details: I asked if they'd help with the fence on Thursday. Got 'we'll see how the week goes.'
That means no. But they'll never say no. They just let it dissolve.
reactions: I'd rather hear 'no' than chase a maybe all week.

entries.intro: {npc_name} remembers things about me that I've forgotten I ever told them.
details: Last week they asked about something I mentioned in passing months ago. I had to think hard to even recall saying it.
It's not just me: they do it with everyone. Like they file away every conversation.
reactions: Being remembered that well is either flattering or slightly worrying. I choose flattering.

entries.intro: {npc_name} is the quiet backbone of this place. Most people don't even see it.
details: They handle the unglamorous work: scheduling, supplies, making sure things don't run out.
Nobody thanks them because nobody notices what doesn't break.
reactions: I try to say thank you when I remember. It's not often enough.

entries.intro: {npc_name} has been dropping hints about something for days and I can't figure out what.
details: Vague comments about 'changes coming' and 'things being different soon.' Always with a knowing look.
I've asked directly and they just smile and say 'you'll see.'
reactions: It's probably nothing important. But the mystery of it is annoying me.

entries.intro: {npc_name} makes this clicking sound with their tongue when they're thinking. I hear it in my sleep.
details: Click click click. Three in a row, then a pause, then three more.
I don't think they're aware of it. I've watched other people flinch at it too.
reactions: Some sounds just get under your skin. This is mine.

entries.intro: {npc_name} wrote down every recipe they know and handed copies out to anyone who'd take one.
details: Pages and pages, all in neat handwriting. Every measurement, every step.
Said they didn't want those recipes to disappear if something happened to them.
reactions: I'm not sure if it's thoughtful or morbid. Maybe both.

entries.intro: {npc_name} somehow always knows the gossip before everyone else.
details: I'll mention something that just happened and they already know. Sometimes they knew yesterday.
I don't know who their sources are, but the network is impressive.
reactions: Useful person to know. But I'm careful what I say around them.

statCheck.pass: They're the kind of person everyone talks to without realizing how much they're revealing. It's the listening, not the asking.
statCheck.fail: I have no idea how they do it. Maybe they just have very good ears.

entries.intro: {npc_name} tried to tell me how to do my own {self_role} work. I've been at it for years.
details: Came over like they'd just invented the wheel and started explaining basics I learned as a child.
I smiled and nodded and waited for it to end. That felt like the polite option.
reactions: Knowing something doesn't mean everyone needs to hear it from you.

entries.intro: {npc_name} stays up later than everyone else, keeping the fire going. I think they do it for all of us.
details: By the time I wake up for my early shift, the embers are still warm. That doesn't happen by accident.
They've never mentioned it. I only noticed because I couldn't sleep one night.
reactions: Invisible work is still work. And it still matters.

entries.intro: I genuinely can't tell if {npc_name} likes living here or hates it.
details: Some days they're talking about improvements they want to make. Other days they're staring at the horizon like they're planning an escape.
When you ask, they give different answers depending on the weather, apparently.
reactions: Most of us feel both ways sometimes. {npc_name} just shows it more.

entries.intro: {npc_name} picks wildflowers on walks and lines them up on the windowsill. Been doing it all season.
details: Different ones each time, arranged in some order I can't figure out.
Nobody asked them to. I don't think they've explained it to anyone either.
reactions: Everyone's got their rituals, I suppose.

entries.intro: {npc_name} has been short with everyone lately. Not just me: everyone.
details: One-word answers, clipped tone, walks away mid-conversation.
I asked {npc_name_2} if they knew what was going on. They didn't.
reactions: Something's eating at them. I just wish they'd let someone help.

statCheck.pass: I've noticed it lines up with the supply shortages. They might be carrying more worry about provisions than they let on.
statCheck.fail: No clue what's behind it. People have their seasons.

entries.intro: {npc_name} keeps their workspace spotless. I've never seen a single thing out of place.
details: Every tool has a spot, every surface is clean. It's almost unsettling how orderly it is.
I tried organizing mine the same way once. Lasted three hours.
reactions: Some people's minds work like that: everything in its place. I'm quietly jealous.

entries.intro: {npc_name} included me when I first arrived here. I hadn't earned it yet. They didn't care.
details: Made introductions, showed me around, sat with me at meals so I wouldn't eat alone.
I was a stranger and they treated me like I'd always been here.
reactions: That kind of welcome shapes how you feel about a place forever.

entries.intro: {npc_name} uses up shared supplies and doesn't replace them. I've noticed.
details: The oil was low yesterday. The salt was out last week. Always after {npc_name} has been through.
It's not malicious: they just take what they need and don't think about what comes next.
reactions: Living together means thinking about the person who comes after you. That's the whole deal.

statCheck.pass: They grew up in a large household where supplies just appeared. Nobody taught them to track what's running low.
statCheck.fail: I don't know if they don't notice or don't care. The outcome's the same.

entries.intro: {npc_name} always cleans up after communal meals. First one clearing plates, last one to sit down.
details: I've never once seen them leave a mess for someone else.
Tried to help once and they shooed me off. Said I'd earned my rest.
reactions: People who clean without being asked are holding everything together.

entries.intro: {npc_name} scratches the back of their neck when they're lying. I've confirmed it three times now.
details: Every single time. Ask them something they don't want to answer honestly: hand goes to neck.
I haven't told anyone else. Feels like useful information to keep.
reactions: Everyone has a tell. Most people just don't watch closely enough.

statCheck.pass: It's a stress response. The body betrays what the mouth won't say. Classic sign.
statCheck.fail: I could be wrong. Maybe they just have an itchy neck.

entries.intro: I caught {npc_name} smiling at nothing the other day. Just standing in the sun, grinning.
details: No one around, nothing happening. Just standing there with this look on their face.
I didn't interrupt. Didn't want to ruin whatever was going on in their head.
reactions: People are strange sometimes. Not in a bad way: just strange.

entries.intro: {npc_name} has no sense of personal space and no sense that they lack it.
details: They'll lean over your shoulder to see what you're working on, breathing on your neck.
I've put my arm out as a barrier. They walked around it.
reactions: I know it's not intentional. That almost makes it worse.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and I don't talk much. But when we do, it picks up like no time has passed.
details: No awkwardness, no explanations. Just straight back into it.
I can't tell if that means we're good friends or just not close enough to notice the gap.
reactions: Some relationships just exist in a middle ground. Comfortable, but hard to label.

entries.intro: {npc_name} tried to barter with me using something that clearly wasn't theirs to trade.
details: I recognized the item. It belongs to someone else here and they definitely didn't give permission.
I didn't take the trade. I didn't say why either. Just shook my head.
reactions: I'm debating whether to mention it to the owner. That's a conversation I don't look forward to.

statCheck.pass: Could be a misunderstanding: maybe they were asked to trade it on someone's behalf. But the way they dodged my eye says otherwise.
statCheck.fail: I might be wrong about whose it is. I'd hate to accuse someone over a mistake.

entries.intro: {npc_name} drew a little map of {settlement_name} for a traveler who was lost. Took real time with it.
details: Marked every building, every path, even where the good mushrooms grow.
The traveler looked genuinely moved. You could tell they hadn't expected that kind of care from a stranger.
reactions: Hospitality like that is what puts a settlement on people's good side.

entries.intro: {npc_name} talks about the old days like they were better. Every single time.
details: 'It used to be this way, it used to be that way.' As if the past was a golden age and we ruined it.
I've been here the same amount of time. The old days were harder, not better.
reactions: Memory has a way of sanding down the rough edges. Doesn't make it true.

entries.intro: {npc_name} offered to cover for me so I could take a day to just do nothing. Nobody does that.
details: Didn't ask what I needed the time for. Just said 'go, I'll handle it.'
I didn't even realize how much I needed it until I had it.
reactions: Recognizing when someone needs rest before they ask: that's a gift.

entries.intro: {npc_name} points out problems but never offers solutions. It's exhausting.
details: 'The path's eroding. The well cap is loose. The woodpile's low.' Yes: we know.
Identifying the issue takes ten seconds. Fixing it takes hours. I know which part {npc_name} prefers.
reactions: I've started responding with 'great, let's fix it together.' The silence afterward says everything.

entries.intro: {npc_name} always knows when I need to be left alone. Never takes it personally.
details: They'll start to come over, read the look on my face, and just give a nod from a distance.
Next day they check in like nothing happened. No guilt, no questions.
reactions: Respecting someone's space without being told: that's real emotional skill.

entries.intro: {npc_name} spent twenty minutes telling me about a dream they had. I still don't know why.
details: Something about a river and a door and a very specific type of bread. I couldn't follow it.
They looked at me expectantly when they finished, like it was supposed to mean something.
reactions: I nodded and said 'interesting.' What else can you do?

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} have started finishing each other's sentences. It's either sweet or terrifying.
details: One starts a thought and the other lands it. Perfectly. Without hesitation.
I've been watching for cracks in it: signals, looks. Nothing. They're just that in sync.
reactions: I don't know whether to be impressed or concerned.

entries.intro: You'd think being the {npc_role} would humble {npc_name} a bit. If anything it's made them worse.
details: Every conversation circles back to their work like it's the most important thing happening.
The rest of us have jobs too. We just don't build shrines to them.
reactions: I try to be patient. Some people just need their work to be seen.

statCheck.pass: I think the role is the only thing that gives them a sense of purpose. Without it, they'd feel invisible.
statCheck.fail: I don't know what drives the ego. Some people just have it.

entries.intro: {npc_name} found a bird with a broken wing and spent a week nursing it back to health.
details: Built a little nest out of rags, fed it by hand, kept it warm near the fire.
When it finally flew off, I swear they looked both happy and heartbroken.
reactions: The way someone treats a helpless thing says everything about them.

entries.intro: {npc_name} is the type who has to have the last word. Every conversation, every disagreement.
details: You can walk away and they'll follow you for three steps to deliver one more point.
I've tried agreeing with them just to end it. They still find something to add.
reactions: I've started counting. My record is seven 'last words' in a single exchange.

entries.intro: {npc_name} makes everyone feel like the most important person in the room when they're talking to you.
details: Full attention. No glancing around, no fidgeting. Just you and them and whatever you're saying.
I've seen them give the same focus to a child's rambling story as to a serious discussion.
reactions: Attention like that is rarer than gold and worth more.

entries.intro: I watched {npc_name} throw out perfectly good food because it wasn't 'fresh enough.' Meanwhile some of us stretch every meal.
details: A whole pot of soup. Barely a day old. Gone.
I didn't say anything in the moment but it's been gnawing at me since.
reactions: Waste like that is hard to stomach when you know what scarcity feels like.

statCheck.pass: I think they got sick from old food once and overcorrect now. Fear does that: makes you throw out what's still good.
statCheck.fail: I don't understand the logic. Food is food.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and I played a game of stones the other night. They let me win and thought I wouldn't notice.
details: Obvious misplays, bad trades they'd never make normally. I saw every one.
I think they were trying to be kind. It just felt patronizing.
reactions: I'd rather lose honestly than win on charity.

statCheck.pass: I think they assumed I needed the boost. Good intention, poor read of the situation.
statCheck.fail: Maybe they were just tired. I could be overthinking it.

entries.intro: {npc_name} left a jar of honey at my door after I had a rough week. No note, no fuss.
details: I knew it was them because nobody else keeps bees around here.
Didn't bring it up later. Didn't need thanks. Just wanted me to have something sweet.
reactions: Kindness like that restores something in you.

entries.intro: I get the sense {npc_name} doesn't think much of the way things are run around here.
details: They never say it outright. Just a raised eyebrow, a pause before agreeing, a tone that's a half-shade off.
It's the kind of criticism that never comes into the open where you can actually address it.
reactions: If you have something to say, say it. The silent judgment is worse.

statCheck.pass: They're comparing {settlement_name} to wherever they came from. The comparison doesn't favor us.
statCheck.fail: Could be reading into nothing. Expressions aren't evidence.

entries.intro: {npc_name} has this ability to calm an argument just by walking into the room.
details: I've seen it happen twice. Voices raised, tension thick, and then they show up and everyone lowers their volume.
It's not authority: it's presence. They just carry themselves in a way that cools things down.
reactions: I wish I had that. Some people are just peacemakers by nature.

entries.intro: {npc_name} compares everyone to people they used to know. 'You remind me of so-and-so' all the time.
details: Apparently I remind them of three different people depending on the day.
I don't want to be compared. I want to be known as myself.
reactions: I think they miss where they came from more than they admit.

entries.intro: {npc_name} stood between me and trouble once. Didn't think twice about it.
details: A disagreement with a passing trader was getting heated and {npc_name} just stepped in. Calm, firm, not aggressive.
The whole thing dissolved in a minute. I don't know what would have happened without them.
reactions: Some people step forward when everyone else steps back. That's {npc_name}.

entries.intro: {npc_name} stacks the communal woodpile in a way that makes it impossible for anyone else to pull from.
details: Everything's wedged in tight at the wrong angle. You pull one log and the whole thing shifts.
I've restacked it twice. By the next day it's back to the impossible arrangement.
reactions: I don't know if it's deliberate or just a profound misunderstanding of how wood piles work.

entries.intro: {npc_name} asked about my family the other day. Nobody here ever asks about my family.
details: Wanted to know if I had siblings, where I grew up, whether I missed home.
I talked for longer than I should have. It felt good to remember out loud.
reactions: Some questions open doors you didn't know were closed.

entries.intro: {npc_name} does this thing where they agree with you to your face and then do the exact opposite.
details: Nods along, says 'makes sense,' then goes and does whatever they were going to do anyway.
I've watched it happen to three different people this week. It's a system.
reactions: I'd rather have someone argue with me than pretend to agree.

entries.intro: {npc_name} keeps a little garden patch behind their house that's the envy of the whole settlement.
details: Everything grows for them. Same soil, same rain as the rest of us, twice the yield.
I've asked for tips and they share freely. It just never works the same for me.
reactions: Some people have green hands. The rest of us just have dirt.

statCheck.pass: I've watched their routine: it's the timing. They water at dawn and mulch at specific intervals. Precision makes the difference.
statCheck.fail: I swear it's sorcery. Nothing else explains it.

entries.intro: {npc_name} has been working themselves to the bone and won't accept help from anyone.
details: Dark circles, short temper, skipping meals. Everyone can see it.
I offered to take on half their load and got a flat 'no' before I finished the sentence.
reactions: Stubbornness dressed up as self-reliance is still stubbornness.

statCheck.pass: I think accepting help feels like failure to them. It's pride, and it's going to cost them if they don't bend.
statCheck.fail: I don't know what drives them to refuse. Maybe it's a trust thing.

entries.intro: {npc_name} said 'I'm proud of you' to me after I finished the wall repair. I wasn't ready for that.
details: It caught me completely off guard. I don't hear that kind of thing very often.
It was just a wall. But the way they said it made it feel like more.
reactions: Words like that from the right person at the right time can carry you for weeks.

entries.intro: I caught {npc_name} reading through the supply list and making changes without telling anyone.
details: Scratched out some items, added others. Just unilaterally decided what we needed.
When I asked about it they said they were 'adjusting priorities.' Whose priorities?
reactions: If we're making decisions together, they need to actually be made together.

entries.intro: {npc_name} helped me replant my entire herb bed after the frost took everything. Spent the whole day at it.
details: Their knees were in the mud same as mine. Didn't leave until the last seedling was in.
When we were done they looked at the rows and said 'that'll do nicely.' Understatement of the year.
reactions: People who show up with their hands, not just their words: those are the ones you keep.

entries.intro: {npc_name} has been giving me the cold shoulder ever since I disagreed with them about the water route.
details: It was a practical discussion. I had a different view. That should be allowed.
Apparently not. Three days of silence over irrigation.
reactions: If a relationship can't survive a disagreement, it wasn't much of one.

entries.intro: {npc_name} keeps finding excuses to walk past {npc_name_2}'s place. I've counted: three times today.
details: Once could be a coincidence. Twice is a stretch. Three times is a route change.
I haven't said anything to either of them. It's more fun to watch.
reactions: People are transparent when they don't know they're being observed.

entries.intro: {npc_name} is the most consistently kind person I know. Not flashy about it: just steady.
details: Every day, every interaction. A kind word, a small gesture, an offered hand. Without exception.
I've watched for a bad day, a slip, anything. It doesn't come.
reactions: Consistency is the hardest part of being good. {npc_name} makes it look easy.

entries.intro: {npc_name} laughed at something I said in front of everyone. Not with me: at me.
details: I'd mispronounced something and they repeated it loudly like it was the funniest thing they'd heard all week.
The table went quiet and {npc_name} didn't seem to notice or care.
reactions: A laugh at someone's expense stops being a joke the moment they're not laughing.

entries.intro: I think {npc_name} and I could be better friends if we tried. We just haven't made the effort.
details: We get along fine when we cross paths. Pleasant, easy. But neither of us ever seeks the other out.
I've thought about inviting them for tea but keep putting it off for no good reason.
reactions: Friendships don't always need a spark. Sometimes they just need someone to take the first step.

entries.intro: When I was going through a hard stretch, {npc_name} left food at my door every morning for a week.
details: I never saw them do it. Just woke up and it was there: bread, fruit, sometimes a jar of something warm.
I cried the third morning. Not from sadness: from being looked after.
reactions: Some debts you repay by passing them on.

entries.intro: {npc_name} plays favorites with information. Certain people know things the rest of us don't.
details: Plans get discussed in little huddles. By the time the rest of us hear about it, decisions are already made.
When you ask why you weren't told, you get a shrug and 'it came up naturally.'
reactions: Information is power, and some people hoard it. {npc_name} is one of them.

statCheck.pass: They build a circle of trust and use it to control the flow of news. It's not accidental: it's how they stay central.
statCheck.fail: Maybe it's not deliberate. Maybe some people are just better at being in the right place.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} have been trading recipes all week. The kitchen's become their territory.
details: New spices, different preparations. Last night's stew had something in it I couldn't identify.
They've taken over the cooking schedule without exactly asking.
reactions: The food's different. Whether it's better depends on who you ask.

entries.intro: {npc_name} stared at me during the whole meeting and didn't contribute a single word.
details: Arms crossed, eyes fixed on me like they were keeping score.
When it was over they left without comment. I have no idea what that was about.
reactions: Silence can be louder than words. And more unsettling.

entries.intro: {npc_name} collects smooth stones. Has a whole shelf of them, all different colors.
details: They pick them up on walks and hold each one up to the light before deciding to keep it.
I asked what makes a stone worth keeping and they said 'you just know.' Very helpful.
reactions: Odd hobbies are the best kind. At least it's harmless.

entries.intro: {npc_name} mended my coat and left it on the hook without a word. I only noticed because the stitching style was different.
details: Neat work too. Better than I could have done myself.
I thanked them and they looked embarrassed, like they'd been caught doing something silly.
reactions: Quiet acts of care are worth more than grand gestures.

entries.intro: I think {npc_name} resents how much work I get done. I've seen the looks.
details: When someone mentions my progress, their jaw tightens. Barely noticeable, but it's there.
They've never said a hostile word. They don't have to.
reactions: I'm not going to slow down to make someone else comfortable. But it makes things awkward.

statCheck.pass: They measure themselves against everyone around them. It's not about me specifically: I just happen to be visible.
statCheck.fail: I could be wrong. Body language is easy to misread.

entries.intro: {npc_name} tells stories about their own mistakes like they're proud of them.
details: Had us laughing about the time they built a shelf upside down and didn't notice for a week.
I can't tell if it's genuine humility or just a way to control the narrative.
reactions: Either way, it works. People warm up to someone who doesn't take themselves too seriously.

entries.intro: {npc_name} has been whispering with {npc_name_2} a lot lately and they go quiet when anyone approaches.
details: It could be nothing. People have private conversations. But the timing feels deliberate.
Both of them have been acting slightly off since it started.
reactions: I'm telling myself it's not about me. Mostly I believe that.

statCheck.pass: I think they're planning something for the settlement: possibly a surprise for someone. The secrecy has that flavor.
statCheck.fail: I have no clue. Could be anything from a gift to a grievance.

entries.intro: {npc_name} is proof that good people exist. Plain and simple.
details: No agenda, no score-keeping. They're kind because it's how they're built.
I've watched them long enough to know it's not an act. Some people are just decent to the bone.
reactions: Hold onto people like that. They're the reason the rest of us keep trying.
